description: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

attacks.name: Gigaspark
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Ampharos

description: By keeping still and listening intently, it can tell what is in even wild, fast-moving rivers.

attacks.name: Bubble Shower
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Azumarill

description: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

attacks.name: Sweet Nectar
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, remove all damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flower Dance
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage times the number of cards with Bellossom in their names that you have in play (including this one).

name: Bellossom

description: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

abilities.name: Berserk
abilities.effect: When you play Feraligatr from your hand, flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards from your opponent's deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards from your deck.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of damage counters on Feraligatr. This attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Feraligatr

description: It is hard for it to support its own weight out of water, so it gets down on all fours. But it moves fast.

attacks.name: Riptide
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 damage times the number of Energy cards in your discard pile. Then, shuffle all Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

name: Feraligatr

description: This powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies' bellies, then lifts and throws them.

abilities.name: Final Blow
abilities.effect: If Heracross's remaining HP are 20 or less, you may make its Megahorn attack's base damage 120 instead of 60. This power can't be used if Heracross is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Megahorn
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Heracross

description: Once it catches the wind, it deftly controls its cotton-puff spores to float, even around the world.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: If this attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Jumpluff, if it has any.

name: Jumpluff

description: It is said that it usually hides in underwater caves. It can create whirlpools by yawning.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Kingdra.

attacks.name: Dragon Tornado
attacks.effect: If this attack doesn't Knock Out the Defending Pokémon, and if there are any Pokémon on your opponent's Bench, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

name: Kingdra

description: It is said that it quietly spends its time deep at the bottom of the sea, because its powers are too strong.

attacks.name: Elemental Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Energy card, a Energy card, and a Energy card attached to Lugia in order to use this attack.

name: Lugia

description: Meganium's breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

abilities.name: Herbal Scent
abilities.effect: When you play Meganium from your hand, you may flip a coin. If heads, remove all damage counters from all Grass Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Meganium

description: The aroma that rises from its petals contains a substance that calms aggressive feelings.

abilities.name: Wild Growth
abilities.effect: As long as Meganium is in play, each Grass Energy card attached to your Grass Pokémon instead provides GrassGrass. This power stops working while Meganium is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Meganium

description: Despite its small size, it can zap even adult humans. However, if it does so, it also surprises itself.

attacks.name: Zzzap
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon in play that has a Pokémon Power. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

name: Pichu

description: Its sturdy wings look heavy, but they are actually hollow and light, allowing it to fly freely in the sky.

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, all damage done by attacks to Skarmory during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Skarmory

description: When its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke's head and unlocked an extraordinary power.

abilities.name: Mind Games
abilities.effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card, you may flip a coin. If heads, that card does nothing. Put it on top of your opponent's deck. This power can't be used if Slowking is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mind Blast
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, this attack does 20 damage.

name: Slowking

description: Its body has been compressed deep under the ground. As a result, it is even harder than a diamond.

attacks.name: Tail Crush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

name: Steelix

description: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

attacks.name: Super Metronome
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack of 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Super Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else in order to use that attack.) (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Togetic's type is still .) Togetic performs that attack. (Togetic can make that attack even if it does not have the appropriate number or type of Energy attached to it necessary to make the attack.)

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Togetic; if tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

name: Togetic

description: If its rage peaks, it becomes so hot that anything that touches it will instantly go up in flames.

abilities.name: Fire Recharge
abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Fire Pokémon. This power can't be used if Typhlosion is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Flame Burst
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and does 20 damage to Typhlosion. If tails, this attack does 60 damage.

name: Typhlosion

description: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

abilities.name: Fire Boost
abilities.effect: When you play Typhlosion from your hand, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards and attach them to Typhlosion. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Flame Wheel
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy cards attached to Typhlosion in order to use this attack. Do 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Typhlosion

effect:
Damage done to the Pokémon Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If the Pokémon Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal, whenever it damages a Pokémon, reduce that damage by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)
name: Metal Energy

description: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

attacks.name: Eeeeeeek
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 7 cards.

name: Cleffa

description: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Donphan.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with his or her Active Pokémon, then, if you have any Benched Pokémon, you switch 1 of them with your Active Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

name: Donphan

description: It rotates its arms to generate electricity, but it tires easily, so it charges up only a little bit.

abilities.name: Playful Punch
abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, do 20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (Apply Weakness and Resistance.) Either way, this ends your turn. This power can't be used if Elekid is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

name: Elekid

description: Each and every time it inhales and exhales, hot embers dribble out of its mouth and nostrils.

attacks.name: Sputter
attacks.effect: All Pokémon Powers stop working until the end of your next turn.

name: Magby

description: It is said that when chased, it lures its attacker onto dark mountain trails where the foe will get lost.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat as long as Murkrow remains your Active Pokémon. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Feint Attack
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Murkrow

description: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Beat Up
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each of your Pokémon in play (including this one). This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Sneasel

description: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

attacks.name: Pilfer
attacks.effect: Shuffle Aipom and all cards attached to it into your deck. Flip a coin. If heads, shuffle a card from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Aipom

description: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It's hard to tell which end is which.

attacks.name: Spider Web
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. (Benching or evolving that Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Poison Bite
attacks.effect: If this attack damages the Defending Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and you remove a number of damage counters from Ariados equal to half that damage (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Ariados has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

name: Ariados

description: A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, Bayleef can't become Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. (All other effects of attacks, Pokémon Powers, and Trainer cards still happen.)

name: Bayleef

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads and if any of your Pokémon have any damage counters on them, then remove 2 damage counters from 1 of them (or 1 if it only has 1). If tails and if any of your opponent's Pokémon have any damage counters on them, choose 1 of them and remove 2 damage counters from it (or 1 if it only has 1).

attacks.name: Double Razor Leaf
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Bayleef

description: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Squaredance
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the total number of Pokémon in play. For each heads, you may search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.effect: Until the end of your next turn, if an attack damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Croconaw

description: It opens its huge jaws wide when attacking. If it loses any fangs while biting, they grow back in.

attacks.name: Sweep Away
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards from your deck.

name: Croconaw

attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused; if tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage and, until the end of your opponent's next turn, as long as Gloom is your Active Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat, and if the effect of an attack, Pokémon Power, or Trainer card would change that player's Active Pokémon, that part of the effect does nothing. If tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage plus 10 damage for each damage counter on Granbull. Then, Granbull does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Floodlight
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Lanturn

name: Ledian

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

abilities.effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand. If your opponent has any Trainer cards there, choose 1 of them. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck. This power can't be used if Noctowl is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

name: Noctowl

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, then if, during your opponent's next turn, Phanpy would be Knocked Out by an attack, Phanpy isn't Knocked Out and its remaining HP become 10 instead.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Snubbull

description: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

attacks.name: Scary Face
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, until the end of your opponent's next turn the Defending Pokémon can't attack or retreat.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Spinarak

description: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.name: Mystifying Horns
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Stantler

description: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears to be closer to a rock than a plant.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Sudowoodo.

name: Sudowoodo

description: It may drop out of the sky suddenly. If attacked by a Spearow, it violently shakes its leaves.

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, you may attach up to 2 Energy cards from your hand to Sunkern.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Remove a number of damage counters from Sunkern equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Sunkern has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

name: Sunkern

description: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Swinub

description: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Totodile.

name: Totodile

description: Its well-developed jaws are powerful and capable of crushing anything. Even its trainer must be careful.

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Totodile during your opponent's next turn. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Totodile

description: This Pokémon lives in cold water. It leaves the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Wooper

effect: Shuffle your deck, then reveal the top 3 cards of it. If at least 2 of those cards share the same name, put all the ones with that name into your hand and shuffle the rest into your deck. If none of them do, shuffle all 3 into your deck.
name: Arcade Game

effect: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. Whenever an attack, Pokémon Power, or Trainer card discards another player's non-Colorless Energy card from a Pokémon, return that Energy card to its owner's hand. (Energy cards that are discarded when that Pokémon is Knocked Out don't count.)
name: Ecogym

effect: Flip a coin. If heads, shuffle up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
name: Energy Charge

effect: Attach Focus Band to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If the Pokémon Focus Band is attached to would be Knocked Out by your opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, that Pokémon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP become 10 instead. Then, discard Focus Band.
name: Focus Band

effect: Draw 2 cards. Then, shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck.
name: Mary

effect: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. If any of them are Trainer cards, you may show 1 of them to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't play any more Trainer cards this turn.
name: PokéGear

effect: Trade 2 of the other cards in your hand for 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile. If you have fewer than 4 basic Energy cards there, take all of them.
name: Super Energy Retrieval

effect: Your opponent may choose 5 Basic Pokémon, Evolution, and/or basic Energy cards in his or her discard pile. (If your opponent doesn't have that many, he or she chooses all or none of them.) If your opponent chooses any cards, he or she shuffles them into his or her deck. Either way, you may do the same, and you can't play any more Trainer cards this turn.
name: Time Capsule

effect: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 4 cards.
name: Bill's Teleporter

effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's face-down Prizes. Guess whether it is an Energy card, a Trainer card, or a Pokémon (Basic or Evolution) card. Flip the card face up (and leave it face up). If you guessed right, draw 2 cards.
name: Card-Flip Game

effect: Attach Gold Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. At any time between turns, if there are at least 4 damage counters on the Pokémon Gold Berry is attached to, you may remove 4 of them and discard Gold Berry. At the start of each turn, if there are at least 4 damage counters on the Pokémon Gold Berry is attached to, remove 4 of them and discard Gold Berry.
name: Gold Berry

effect: Attach Miracle Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. At any time between turns, if the Pokémon Miracle Berry is attached to is Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, you may remove all those effects from that Pokémon and discard Miracle Berry. At the start of each turn, if the Pokémon Miracle Berry is attached to is Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, remove all of those effects from that Pokémon and discard Miracle Berry.
name: Miracle Berry

effect: Shuffle your deck. Then, look at up to 5 cards from the top of your deck and rearrange them as you like.
name: New Pokédex

effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 7 cards. You can't play any more Trainer cards this turn.
name: Professor Elm

effect: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. All damage done by Colorless Pokémon's attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
name: Sprout Tower

effect: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
name: Super Scoop Up

effect: Attach Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. At any time between turns, if there are at least 2 damage counters on the Pokémon Berry is attached to, you may remove 2 of them and discard Berry. At the start of each turn, if there are at least 2 damage counters on the Pokémon Berry is attached to, remove 2 of them and discard Berry.
name: Berry

effect: If you have any Benched Pokémon, your opponent chooses 1 of them and switches it with your Active Pokémon. Then, if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.
name: Double Gust

effect: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. Remove 2 damage counters times the number of heads from that Pokémon. If the Pokémon has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.
name: Moo-Moo Milk

effect: Your opponent may search his or her deck for 1 Basic Pokémon card and put it onto his or her Bench. Then, you may search your deck for 1 Basic Pokémon card and put it onto your Bench. Then, each player shuffles his or her deck. (A player can't do any of this if his or her Bench is full.)
name: Pokémon March

effect: Flip a coin. If heads, put an Evolution card from your discard pile, if any, into your hand. If tails, put a Basic Pokémon card from your discard pile, if any, into your hand.
name: Super Rod

effect:
If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to damages the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon. At the end of every turn, put 1 damage counter on the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to, unless it's Darkness or has Dark in its name.
Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)
name: Darkness Energy

effect: Recycle Energy provides Colorless Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.) If this card is put into your discard pile from play, return it to your hand.
name: Recycle Energy
